title: Building a design system at Genius Scan | Swift by Sundell
description: An example of how to approach the task of building a design system for an app, by focusing on creating an initial set of reusable components that can be tweaked using the SwiftUI environment.

title: Deciding between ‘let’ and ‘var’ for Swift struct properties | Swift by Sundell
description: Does marking a given Swift struct property as either ‘let’ or ‘var’ simply determine its mutability, or is there more to it? Let’s explore!

title: Decoding Swift types that require additional data | Swift by Sundell
description: How Swift’s CodableWithConfiguration API can be used to inject additional, required data into a type’s decoding process, without having to use optionals or declare any additional decoding-specific types.

title: Tips and tricks for when using SwiftUI’s ViewBuilder | Swift by Sundell
description: Let’s explore how we can use SwiftUI’s ViewBuilder within our own code, and a few things that can be good to keep in mind when doing so.

title: Using Swift’s defer keyword within async and throwing contexts | Swift by Sundell
description: How Swift’s defer keyword can be incredibly useful when working with code scopes that have multiple exit points, such as throwing or async functions.

title: Modern URL construction in Swift | Swift by Sundell
description: How to combine Foundation’s latest URL APIs with Swift features like macros, to be able to work with both static and dynamic URLs in simpler and more predictable ways.

title: Swift by Sundell is back! | Swift by Sundell
description: After a two-year hiatus, Swift by Sundell is finally back, with new articles published on a regular basis.

title: SwiftUI views versus modifiers | Swift by Sundell
description: What’s really the difference between building a piece of UI as a view versus a modifier, and how to choose between those two solutions?

title: Observing the content offset of a SwiftUI ScrollView | Swift by Sundell
description: How the content offset of a SwiftUI ScrollView can be observed without bridging to UIKit.

title: 123: “The evolution of Swift”, with special guest Nick Lockwood | Swift by Sundell
description: On this final episode of 2022, Nick Lockwood returns to the show to discuss the overall evolution of Swift and its ecosystem of tools and libraries. How has Swift changed since its original introduction in 2014, how does it compare to other modern programming languages, and how might the language continue to evolve in 2023 and beyond?

title: 122: “Swift concurrency in practice”, with special guest Ben Scheirman | Swift by Sundell
description: Ben Scheirman returns to the show to discuss how Swift’s built-in concurrency features, such as async/await and tasks, can be used in practice when building apps for Apple’s platforms.

title: Combining opaque return types with primary associated types | Swift by Sundell
description: How Swift’s opaque return types can be combined with primary associated types in order to create powerful abstractions without the need for any type erasure.

title: 121: “Responsive and smooth UIs”, with special guest Adam Bell | Swift by Sundell
description: Adam Bell returns to the podcast to discuss different techniques and approaches for optimizing UI code, and how to utilize tools like animations in order to build iOS apps that feel fast and responsive.

title: 120: “Freelancing and WWDC22 highlights”, with special guest Donny Wals | Swift by Sundell
description: Donny Wals returns to the show to talk about being an iOS developer freelancer, and to discuss some of the key new APIs, Swift language features, and frameworks that were introduced at WWDC22.

title: 119: “The role of system design”, with special guest Gui Rambo | Swift by Sundell
description: Gui Rambo returns to the show to talk about the role and importance of system design when building apps and open source tools, and how common app architectures and design patterns can be augmented with custom systems.

title: Essential Developer | Swift by Sundell
description: Thanks a lot to Essential Developer for sponsoring Swift by Sundell.

title: 118: “What’s new in SwiftUI in iOS 16?”, with special guest Natalia Panferova | Swift by Sundell
description: Natalia Panferova joins John to discuss some of the key new features that are coming to SwiftUI and UIKit in iOS 16, and to talk about her experience working on SwiftUI at Apple.

title: Switching between SwiftUI’s HStack and VStack | Swift by Sundell
description: Different ways to implement a dynamic SwiftUI stack that renders its content either horizontally or vertically, depending on the current context.

title: 117: “Swift 5.7, generics, and the road to Swift 6”, with special guest Ben Cohen | Swift by Sundell
description: Ben Cohen, manager of the Swift team at Apple, joins John on this WWDC22 special to discuss Swift 5.7, how generics have been made more powerful and easy to use, and how the language is expected to evolve towards Swift 6.

title: Using the ‘some’ and ‘any’ keywords to reference generic protocols in Swift 5.7 | Swift by Sundell
description: How Swift 5.7 makes generic protocols more powerful and easier to work with, thanks to the ‘some’ and ‘any’ keywords.

title: Swift 5.7’s new optional unwrapping syntax | Swift by Sundell
description: A quick look at a new, more concise way to unwrap optional values that’s being introduced in Swift 5.7.

title: Rendering SwiftUI views within UITableView or UICollectionView cells on iOS 16 | Swift by Sundell
description: Exploring how iOS 16’s new UIHostingConfiguration API enables us to inline SwiftUI views within our UITableView or UICollectionView cells.

title: 116: “The evolution of SwiftUI”, with special guest Chris Eidhof | Swift by Sundell
description: Chris Eidhof returns to the podcast to talk about how SwiftUI has evolved since its initial release, to share several key learnings from using it over the past few years, and to discuss concepts like app architecture and state management.

title: Swift by Sundell turns five years old today! Here’s what’s next for the website and the podcast | Swift by Sundell
description: Celebrating the fifth birthday of this website, while also sharing some important announcements about its future.

title: 115: “A framework and an app”, with special guest Simon Støvring | Swift by Sundell
description: Simon Støvring returns to the show to talk about how he built his new text editor Runestone, how to effectively manage an app’s settings, performance tuning, and implementing an app’s core logic as a stand-alone framework.

title: Type placeholders in Swift | Swift by Sundell
description: New in Swift 5.6: We can now use type placeholders to select what generic types that we want the compiler to infer. Let’s take a look at how those placeholders work, and what kinds of situations that they could be really useful in.

title: 114: “Accessibility on Apple’s platforms”, with special guest Sommer Panage | Swift by Sundell
description: Sommer Panage returns to the show to discuss Apple’s various accessibility APIs and tools, how to incorporate accessibility support into a team’s overall development workflow, and what it was like being an engineering manager at Apple.

title: Abstract types and methods in Swift | Swift by Sundell
description: What abstract types are, how they could be implemented in Swift, and how they relate to protocols and other polymorphic programming techniques.

title: Sponsor: Judo | Swift by Sundell
description: Thanks a lot to Judo for sponsoring Swift by Sundell.

title: Equality | Swift by Sundell
description: Let’s take a look at how Swift models the concept of equality, and how that concept varies between value and reference types.

title: 113: “Where is Swift headed in 2022?”, with special guest JP Simard | Swift by Sundell
description: On this 2022 season premiere, JP Simard returns to the show to discuss what’s next for Swift in 2022, and what kinds of improvements and new features that might be coming to the language during the year.

title: Writing testable code when using SwiftUI | Swift by Sundell
description: Let’s take a look at how we can make our UI-related logic fully testable, even when that logic is primarily used within SwiftUI-based views.

title: Combining protocols in Swift | Swift by Sundell
description: Let’s take a look at various ways to combine multiple protocols into new functionality, either by using protocol extensions, composition, or by defining dedicated types.

title: Memory management when using async/await in Swift | Swift by Sundell
description: Managing an app’s memory is something that tends to be especially tricky when it comes to asynchronous code, so let’s take a look at how to do just that when using async/await.

title: Automatically retrying an asynchronous Swift Task | Swift by Sundell
description: A few examples on how to use Swift Concurrency to write asynchronous operations that are automatically retried if an error was encountered.

title: Backgrounds and overlays in SwiftUI | Swift by Sundell
description: How SwiftUI enables us to stack views along the Z axis, which in turn makes it possible to create all sorts of backgrounds, overlays, and other effects.

title: Loops | Swift by Sundell
description: A look at many different built-in ways to iterate over arrays, dictionaries, and other Swift collections.

title: Creating Combine-compatible versions of async/await-based APIs | Swift by Sundell
description: Creating convenience APIs that make it possible to convert async/await-based functions into Combine publishers.

title: Two ways of capturing self strongly within a closure | Swift by Sundell
description: How to use either explicit self references or a capture list to capture self strongly within an escaping Swift closure.

title: 112: “The 2021 Season Finale” | Swift by Sundell
description: To wrap up the 2021 season of the show, John revisits some of the key themes and topics that were discussed both on the show itself, and within the Swift community in general, throughout the year.

title: Building an asynchronous SwiftUI button | Swift by Sundell
description: How a custom SwiftUI button that’s capable of running asynchronous actions can be created, and how to make such a control versatile and easy to reuse across a project.

title: 111: “Custom rendering”, with special guest James Thomson | Swift by Sundell
description: James Thomson returns to the show to discuss the various technologies that enable us to render custom UIs on Apple’s platforms. From rendering views using Core Graphics and Core Animation, to building completely custom 3D-based UIs using SceneKit and RealityKit.

title: Discover Concurrency on Swift by Sundell
description: Explore Swift’s built-in concurrency system, and how to use tools like async/await and actors to write concurrent code in robust and efficient ways.

title: Delaying an asynchronous Swift Task | Swift by Sundell
description: How we can use the built-in Task type to delay certain operations when using Swift’s new concurrency system.

title: Lightweight dependency injection and unit testing using async functions | Swift by Sundell
description: Let’s explore how we could make use of Swift’s async/await capabilities to make our asynchronous code fully testable in a very lightweight way.

title: 110: “Concurrency beyond app development”, with special guest Tim Condon | Swift by Sundell
description: Tim Condon joins John to discuss how both client and server-side Swift developers could utilize the new built-in concurrency system, as well as how distributed actors and other upcoming language features might continue to make Swift even more capable on the server.

title: What role do Tasks play within Swift’s concurrency system? | Swift by Sundell
description: How Swift’s new Task type works, and how it enables us to encapsulate, observe, and control the way that our asynchronous code is executed.

title: Swift actors: How do they work, and what kinds of problems do they solve? | Swift by Sundell
description: Let’s explore Swift’s version of actors, and what kind of problems that we could solve by defining custom actor types within our code bases.

title: 109: “Code maintenance”, with special guest Nick Lockwood | Swift by Sundell
description: Nick Lockwood joins John for a discussion about maintaining Swift code bases — from open source projects, to ones worked on by teams of various sizes. Also, the design of ShapeScript, using async/await within unit tests, and much more.

title: Unit testing Swift code that uses async/await | Swift by Sundell
description: Let’s take a look at how to call async APIs within our unit tests, and also how async/await can be a great testing tool in general.

title: Using count vs isEmpty to check whether a collection contains any elements | Swift by Sundell
description: What’s the difference between those two ways, and when should which be used?

title: Building async and concurrent versions of forEach and map | Swift by Sundell
description: Let’s take a look at how we can utilize Swift’s built-in concurrency system when performing data transformations using functions like forEach and map.

title: 108: “Concurrency and Swift-DocC”, with special guest Marin Todorov | Swift by Sundell
description: Marin Todorov returns to the podcast to discuss Swift’s new concurrency system and its newly announced backward compatibility, his new book about that topic, and his work on Apple’s open source documentation tool, Swift-DocC.

title: Using Swift’s concurrency system to run multiple tasks in parallel | Swift by Sundell
description: Let’s take a look at a few different ways to run multiple tasks in parallel when using Swift’s built-in concurrency system, and when each of those techniques can be especially useful.

title: 107: “UIKit in 2021”, with special guest Ben Scheirman | Swift by Sundell
description: Ben Scheirman returns to the show to talk about modern UIKit-based app development, how UIKit has evolved over the past few years, and how its API design and usage has been influenced by the introduction of frameworks like SwiftUI and Combine.

title: Making async system APIs backward compatible | Swift by Sundell
description: How to make it possible to use async system APIs on earlier operating system versions.

title: Swift’s new concurrency system gains backward compatibility in Xcode 13.2 | Swift by Sundell
description: Starting in Xcode 13.2, Swift’s new suite of concurrency features are now backward compatible all the way back to iOS 13, macOS Catalina, watchOS 6, and tvOS 13.

title: Discover Generics on Swift by Sundell
description: Learn how to fully utilize Swift’s powerful generics system to write reusable types, functions, extensions, and protocols.

title: Specialized extensions using generic type constraints | Swift by Sundell
description: How Swift’s generics system enables us to create specialized extensions that only apply to types that fit certain requirements, and how that capability can be really useful.

title: Building editable lists with SwiftUI | Swift by Sundell
description: How SwiftUI’s List and ForEach types can be used to build lists that support inline editing, moving, and deletions, and how we can then build our own abstractions on top of those built-in types.

title: Which of the SwiftUI APIs introduced in iOS 15 are backward compatible? | Swift by Sundell
description: Let’s take a look at which of the new SwiftUI APIs that are backward compatible with earlier OS versions, and what language features that made that possible.

title: 106: “Opportunities for optimization”, with special guest Christian Selig | Swift by Sundell
description: Christian Selig returns to the show to talk about how he used the new Safari extension system on iOS to build Amplosion and Achoo, the pros and cons of open source, and how developers can utilize other iOS 15 and iPhone hardware features.

title: Programmatic navigation in SwiftUI | Swift by Sundell
description: How we can take direct control over SwiftUI’s navigation system, which enables us to dynamically show and hide views within either a TabView or NavigationView.

title: 105: “Building various kinds of editors”, with special guest Marcin Krzyzanowski | Swift by Sundell
description: Marcin Krzyzanowski returns to the show to talk about building editors for Swift code, backend-driven user interfaces, and more. Also, the challenges of working with text-based data, the pros and cons of composition, and managing hobby projects.

title: Previewing SwiftUI views in landscape | Swift by Sundell
description: How to preview a SwiftUI view in landscape using Xcode 13, and how to enable that new feature to be used within projects that also support previous iOS versions.

title: Using Combine’s share operator to avoid duplicate work | Swift by Sundell
description: How the share operator enables a Combine publisher to be reused without requiring duplicate work to be performed for each subscriber.

title: Discover Codable on Swift by Sundell
description: Discover Swift’s Codable protocol, how to fully leverage its compiler synthesis features, and how to use its surrounding APIs to write efficient serialization code.

title: Codable synthesis for Swift enums | Swift by Sundell
description: How Codable’s auto-synthesis works for enums, and how that part of the system has been upgraded in Swift 5.5 to support associated values.

title: Sponsor: Paste | Swift by Sundell
description: Thanks a lot to Paste for sponsoring Swift by Sundell.

title: Conditional compilation within Swift expressions | Swift by Sundell
description: How Swift 5.5 enables us to conditionally compile postfix member expressions using compiler directives, and what kinds of situations that this new feature could be useful in.

title: 104: “The magic of Augmented Reality”, with special guest Roxana Jula | Swift by Sundell
description: Roxana Jula joins John to discuss Apple’s various tools, frameworks, and APIs for building Augmented Reality experiences. How to get started building AR-based apps, what’s the current state of AR on Apple’s platforms, and how might that change if Apple were to introduce dedicated AR hardware in the future?

title: Async sequences, streams, and Combine | Swift by Sundell
description: How Swift’s AsyncSequence and AsyncStream APIs can be used to build asynchronous sequences, and how they relate to Apple’s Combine framework.

title: 103: “What’s new in Swift 5.5”, with special guest Antoine van der Lee | Swift by Sundell
description: Antoine van der Lee, creator of SwiftLee, joins John to discuss the new language features that are being introduced as part of Swift 5.5 — from the brand new concurrency system, to convenience features and various improvements.

title: Using static protocol APIs to create conforming instances | Swift by Sundell
description: How we can now use Swift’s very convenient “dot syntax” to refer to protocol-conforming types, and how that improves some of SwiftUI’s styling APIs.

title: Defining dynamic colors in Swift | Swift by Sundell
description: Various techniques that let us define fully dynamic colors that adapt to the user’s selected color scheme, using either SwiftUI or UIKit.

title: Using ‘@unknown default’ within switch statements | Swift by Sundell
description: What Swift’s @unknown attribute does, and why the compiler tells us to use it when switching on certain enums.

title: 102: “WWDC21, two months later”, with special guest Jordan Morgan | Swift by Sundell
description: Jordan Morgan returns to the show to discuss some of the key new APIs and frameworks that were announced at WWDC21, and how they can be integrated into the apps that we build, now that about two months have passed since the conference took place.

title: Throwing and asynchronous Swift properties | Swift by Sundell
description: How Swift 5.5 enables computed properties to become either throwing or asynchronous, and what sort of situations that these new capabilities could become useful in.

title: Attaching property wrappers to function arguments | Swift by Sundell
description: New in Swift 5.5: Property wrappers can now be applied directly to function arguments, just like how they can be used to add additional functionality to a property or local variable.

title: 101: “Cross-platform SwiftUI”, with special guest Malin Sundberg | Swift by Sundell
description: Malin Sundberg joins John to talk about her experiences of using SwiftUI to build and ship the time-tracking and invoicing app Orbit on most of Apple’s platforms, and what sort of things that can be good to keep in mind when using SwiftUI in a cross-platform context.

title: Stroking and filling a SwiftUI shape at the same time | Swift by Sundell
description: How to compose multiple SwiftUI views to render a shape that’s both stroked and filled at the same time.

title: Mutating and non-mutating Swift contexts | Swift by Sundell
description: What sort of capabilities that a mutating Swift context has, and what the mutating and nonmutating keywords do.

title: Sponsor: ViRE | Swift by Sundell
description: Thanks a lot to ViRE for sponsoring Swift by Sundell.

title: 100: “A tower of capabilities”, with special guest Chris Lattner | Swift by Sundell
description: Chris Lattner returns to the show to discuss Swift’s new concurrency features, the ongoing evolution of the language, and the importance of both language and API design. This, and much more, on this special 100th episode of the show.

title: Dismissing a SwiftUI modal or detail view | Swift by Sundell
description: Three different ways to dismiss a SwiftUI view that was presented from another view, including a new API that’s being introduced in iOS 15.

title: Calling async functions within a Combine pipeline | Swift by Sundell
description: Let’s explore how we can make it possible to call async/await-powered APIs within a Combine pipeline.

title: Making SwiftUI views refreshable | Swift by Sundell
description: A look at how SwiftUI’s new refreshable modifier can be used to either add pull-to-refresh to a list, or to build completely custom reloading UIs and logic.

title: Availability checks | Swift by Sundell
description: Availability checks let us conditionally use new system APIs and features while still enabling the rest of our code to keep running on older system versions. Let’s take a look at how they can be used.

title: Customizing how an external Swift type is encoded or decoded | Swift by Sundell
description: Various ways to handle mismatches between how an external type is expected to be coded and the data format that an app is using.

title: Connecting async/await to other Swift code | Swift by Sundell
description: Let’s explore a few ways to “bridge the gap” between the new world of async/await and other kinds of asynchronous Swift code.

title: Automatic conversions between Double and CGFloat values in Swift 5.5 | Swift by Sundell
description: How the Swift compiler is now able to automatically perform conversions between Double and CGFloat values.

title: Using the MainActor attribute to automatically dispatch UI updates on the main queue | Swift by Sundell
description: How the MainActor attribute eliminates the need for us to manually dispatch UI updates on the main queue when using Swift 5.5’s new concurrency system.

title: 99: “Structured concurrency”, with special guest Doug Gregor | Swift by Sundell
description: Doug Gregor from Apple joins John to discuss Swift 5.5’s new concurrency features in great detail. How do features like async/await and actors work under the hood, and how were those concepts adapted in order to feel right at home within Swift’s existing ecosystem? That, and much more, on this WWDC21 special episode of the show.

title: WWDC by Sundell & Friends
description: Daily coverage of WWDC, by John Sundell and friends.

title: A Swift developer’s WWDC dreams - 2021 edition | Swift by Sundell
description: With the start of WWDC21 just around the corner, I’d like to share some of my biggest hopes and dreams for this year’s edition of the conference.

title: 98: “An entire spectrum of apps”, with special guest Sean Allen | Swift by Sundell
description: Sean Allen returns to the show to talk about using the MVVM design pattern within SwiftUI-based apps, building a course around MapKit and CloudKit, how to approach all of the new material that WWDC has to offer, and what goes into making a really great developer portfolio.

title: Avoiding problematic cases when using Swift enums | Swift by Sundell
description: Swift’s enums are awesome, but they’re not always the best choice for modeling a given piece of data. Let’s explore why that is, and what other tools that can be good to keep in mind in order to avoid certain problematic enum cases.

title: 97: “Anything can happen during WWDC”, with special guest Ish ShaBazz | Swift by Sundell
description: Ish ShaBazz returns to the show for a special pre-WWDC episode about what new APIs and developer tools that Apple might announce at this year’s edition of the conference. What’s in store for Swift and SwiftPM, how might UIKit and SwiftUI continue to evolve, and will this be the year when we’ll finally see Apple’s new AR/VR platform?

title: Avoiding having to recompute values within SwiftUI views | Swift by Sundell
description: When using multiple computed properties within SwiftUI views could become problematic, and various approaches to fix those kinds of problems.

title: Three ways to render a SwiftUI view in a playground | Swift by Sundell
description: Great options for when we don’t want to create a whole new Xcode project just to try out a quick UI idea.
